title: Find the First Duplicate in an Unsorted Array

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, find the first duplicate in the array. The array may contain duplicate elements, and the order of elements is not guaranteed to be in ascending or descending order. If no duplicate is found, return -1.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an array of integers, e.g. [2, 4, 3, 5, 2, 1]
statement.outputFormat: The output should be the first duplicate element found in the array, e.g. 2
statement.notes: The array length will be between 1 and 10000, and all elements will be between -10000 and 10000. You can use any data structure to solve this problem.

examples.explanation: The first duplicate element in the array is 2.
There is no duplicate element in the array, so return -1.
The first duplicate element in the array is 2.

hints.text: You can use a HashSet to store unique elements and find the first duplicate.

editorial.title: Find the First Duplicate in an Unsorted Array
editorial.intuition: The problem can be solved by using a HashSet to store unique elements and find the first duplicate.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach: Iterate through the array and check if each element is in the hash set. If it is, return the element. If not, add it to the hash set.
editorial.betterSolution: Better solution: Use a HashSet to store unique elements and find the first duplicate.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution: Use a HashSet to store unique elements and find the first duplicate. Iterate through the array and check if each element is in the hash set. If it is, return the element. If not, add it to the hash set.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correct solution can be proven using the properties of HashSets.
commonMistakes: Not using a HashSet to store unique elements.
Not checking if an element is in the hash set before returning it.
